jobs.description: Read this first. We're a fast-growing startup looking for a long-term email marketing partner, not a one-off gig. If you do great work with us, this role grows — more hours, higher rate, bigger scope, and a real seat at the table as the brand scales. We'd much rather find one person we can build with for years than burn through short-term hires. If you're looking for a stable, long-term engagement where your impact is directly visible in revenue, keep reading. Who we are. We're Futstrikers, a youth soccer ball brand on Shopify (futstrikersclub.com) with real momentum — growing customer base, expanding product line, an active membership program, and wholesale accounts coming online. We sell match balls DTC to players, families, and clubs, and we also run wholesale through the same storefront with segmented customer accounts. We have an in-house team (graphic designer, full-stack developers, brand leadership) and we're hiring on the marketing side because email is the next growth lever. The brand is past the "is this going to work" stage and into the "how fast can we scale it" stage. You'd be coming in at the exact right moment to own a channel from the ground up and grow your role alongside it. What we need. We're looking for an experienced Klaviyo marketer to own our email channel end-to-end — strategy, flow build, copy, segmentation, campaigns, and ongoing optimization. Our goal is simple: grow email-attributed revenue and expand our active subscriber base. Klaviyo will be connected to Shopify and ready to go before you start. You won't be setting up the account or importing lists — you'll be stepping into a live Klaviyo account, building out the segmentation architecture, designing and launching flows, writing copy, and running campaigns. Transactional and account emails (order confirmation, shipping, account creation) currently run on Shopify defaults, and we'll want your input on whether any of those are worth moving into Klaviyo for brand consistency down the road — but that's a judgment call, not an immediate task. Here's the flow library we need, at a high level. On the DTC side: welcome and onboarding flows for new subscribers and first-time customers; a membership conversion flow that promotes the Futstrikers membership ($19.99 per year, which gets members 25% off all balls plus free shipping) to email subscribers who haven't purchased and to one-time buyers who aren't yet members; a member reorder and replenishment flow built around themes like "lost ball," "more training equals more balls," new season, and tournament prep, to keep active members buying; a non-member reorder flow that uses the same reorder logic for single purchasers, with membership itself positioned as the upsell; membership renewal reminders and a lapsed-member win-back; browse abandonment and abandoned checkout; post-purchase and review requests; and announcement and product-launch campaigns. On the wholesale side: reorder reminder flows tuned to B2B cadence (clubs, coaches, and resellers typically reorder seasonally, so flows should reflect that rhythm, not DTC timing); tier-based incentive campaigns, because our wholesale customers sit on different pricing tiers and campaigns need to be segmented so each tier sees the right offer and message; wholesale onboarding and account nurture; B2B announcements for new products, restocks, and seasonal buy windows; and a win-back flow for wholesale accounts that haven't reordered. Beyond flows, you'll own segmentation strategy — cleanly separating DTC and wholesale on a shared storefront, and inside each side segmenting further by membership status (member, non-member, lapsed), wholesale pricing tier, lifecycle stage, product interest, and order cadence. You'll handle copywriting for all flows and campaigns. Our brand voice is direct and youth-soccer-first, not corporate. You'll run the campaign calendar, planning and executing weekly or biweekly sends around drops, restocks, tournaments, and seasonality. You'll deliver weekly and monthly performance recaps covering attributed revenue, flow performance, and next-step recommendations. And you'll own deliverability and list hygiene to keep us in the inbox. What we provide on our end. We have an in-house graphic designer — you write the brief, they produce the visuals. We have in-house full-stack developers, so any technical work (API, Shopify theme, tracking, custom blocks, webhook and event wiring) is handled by our team. Klaviyo is already set up and integrated with Shopify, with our list loaded and ready. Shopify is live with DTC and wholesale segmentation already in place. Brand assets, product imagery, and CDN-hosted visuals are ready for you to use. You focus on strategy, flow architecture, segmentation, copy, and campaigns; we handle design and dev. Who we're looking for. You should have 3+ years of hands-on Klaviyo experience (please share a portfolio or example flows). You should have demonstrable experience with Shopify and Klaviyo working together — segmentation, flow logic, event-driven triggers, and revenue reporting. Experience segmenting DTC and B2B or wholesale audiences on a single storefront is a major plus. You should be a strong ecommerce copywriter — please send 2 to 3 examples of flow emails you've personally written. You should be comfortable working independently against revenue targets. Bonus points for sports, apparel, consumer-goods, or subscription and membership brand experience. How to apply. In your proposal, please include four things. First, 2 to 3 Klaviyo accounts or brands you've worked on and the outcomes — attributed revenue lift, list growth, flow performance. Second, links or screenshots of flow emails you personally wrote and built. Third, your take on the biggest levers for (a) driving membership signups from our existing DTC list and (b) building tier-specific wholesale reorder flows — what would you build first and why? Fourth, your availability in hours per week and confirmation that you can be reachable on Slack during our work hours. Engagement details. This is an ongoing hourly contract starting at $5 to $7 per hour, capped at 20 to 25 hours per week, with an expectation of roughly 3 to 4 hours of focused work per day, Monday through Friday. You must be available on Slack during our work hours, which are 9am to 2pm and 10pm to midnight Arizona time (MST, UTC-7, no daylight saving). You don't need to be working the entire window — but you need to be reachable on Slack during those hours for quick questions, approvals, and handoffs with our in-house designer and dev team. Why the starting rate. We're being upfront: the starting rate reflects where we are as a growing startup, not where this role is going. The plan is to prove fit together over the first couple of months and then step the rate up as email-attributed revenue grows. We reward performance — the person in this seat grows with the company. If you ship great work, you will not be stuck at the starting rate. Please start your proposal with the word "Flowstate" so we know you read this. We're looking for an ongoing engagement, 6+ months minimum, with someone who wants to plant a flag and grow with a brand. You're walking into a live Klaviyo account, a live Shopify store, a supportive in-house team, and a brand with real momentum — your job is to turn that into email-driven revenue. If you're the kind of marketer who would rather own a channel inside a scaling company than bounce between one-off projects, this is your gig. Apply.
jobs.title: Klaviyo Email Marketer for Shopify Brand (Flows + Copy + Segmentation)

jobs.description: The short version We're a screen print and embroidery shop in Ann Arbor, MI (No Mids, est. 2023) and we need a custom product designer tool built into our Shopify store. Customers pick a blank, upload or choose artwork, see a live mockup on the selected garment/color, and watch pricing update as they adjust quantity, placement, and decoration method. At checkout, Shopify authorizes the card but doesn't capture — we review each order manually before charging. We already have design direction, architecture decisions, a working React prototype, and a clear data flow mapped out. We're not looking for someone to figure out the problem from scratch — we're looking for someone to execute a well-defined build. Open to hourly for the right person. Timeline: 4–6 weeks, ideally starting within the next 2 weeks. Who we are No Mids is a small but growing custom apparel studio. We print and embroider for brands, bands, breweries, and businesses that care about how their merch looks. Our aesthetic direction is already locked in — think Off-White meets 90s skate zine. Platform: Shopify (we're not open to moving off it) Product import: Supply Master, pulling from SanMar and S&S Activewear APIs Catalog size: thousands of SKUs across t-shirts, hoodies, crewnecks, polos, caps, and accessories Theme: [CONFIRM YOUR THEME HERE — Dawn / Impulse / Broadcast / Custom] Payment capture: will be set to manual (authorization hold, manual capture after review) What needs to get built 1. A custom Shopify theme section — the "Studio" design tool Lives on product pages. When a customer lands on any decorated product, they see: The current variant image (color-specific, already imported by Supply Master) A canvas overlay where their artwork renders at the correct placement zone Controls for: decoration method (screen print / DTG / embroidery), number of print colors or stitch count, placement (front center / left chest / back full / sleeve), quantity Live-updating price display that recalculates on every input change, showing per-piece and total Artwork upload (accepts PNG, JPG, SVG, PDF, AI — stored in Shopify Files or an S3 bucket you set up) An "add to cart" button that packages all customization data as Shopify line item properties 2. A template-per-garment-type architecture — NOT per-variant This is the single most important technical requirement. We previously tried a tool that required us to create a mockup template for every individual product and variant, which was unworkable with thousands of imported SKUs. The right architecture is: ~12 garment templates total (unisex tee, fitted tee, pullover hoodie, zip hoodie, crewneck, polo, trucker cap, dad hat, beanie, tote, etc.) Each template is a JSON config defining print zones as normalized coordinates (0.0–1.0) relative to the product photo Products get assigned a template via a Shopify tag (e.g., garment:tee-unisex) applied automatically during Supply Master import based on category The configurator reads the tag, loads the matching template, and pulls the variant-specific photo from Shopify's product data — no per-SKU setup needed Brand-level offsets allowed for suppliers whose photography is consistently shifted (e.g., SanMar vs S&S having slightly different chest heights) If you've done this coordinate-normalization approach before, please mention it. If you haven't, please at least acknowledge in your proposal that you understand why this matters. 3. A live pricing engine Industry-standard screen print, DTG, and embroidery pricing logic: Screen print: per-color, per-location charge with quantity tier breaks (12–23, 24–47, 48–71, 72–143, 144–287, 288+) plus per-color setup fees DTG: per-piece flat rate with quantity tier breaks, no setup Embroidery: per-1,000-stitches rate with quantity tier breaks plus digitizing setup fee Blank cost: pulled from the Shopify variant price (which Supply Master keeps synced), with a configurable markup percentage applied All rates and tiers need to be editable from an admin-accessible config file or settings panel — not hardcoded — because we'll tweak these regularly The calculator also shows a price-break ladder so customers see how the per-piece price drops at higher quantities — this is a huge conversion tool for our category. 4. Cart handoff via Shopify line item properties When added to cart, the line item must include (as properties): Artwork file URL (or array of URLs if multiple placements) Mockup preview image URL (for admin review) Placement zone Decoration method Color count (for screen print) or stitch count (for embroidery) Per-piece print charge and setup fee (broken out for admin clarity) Any customer notes Properties with an underscore prefix (_artwork_url) to keep them hidden from the customer cart display but visible in admin. 5. Manual capture + admin order view polish We'll configure Shopify Payments for manual capture ourselves. What we need from you: Ensure the order admin view cleanly displays all line item properties (artwork preview thumbnail, specs, pricing breakdown) so our team can review quickly Ideally: a small admin-side helper that generates a print-ready file package (artwork + spec sheet) per order. This is nice-to-have, not required for v1. What we're providing A working React prototype demonstrating the full customer flow, aesthetic direction — you can reference it throughout the build A written build spec with architecture decisions, data flow diagrams, and template config format All visual design assets — fonts, colors, the NO MIDS wordmark, CSS variables Template coordinate measurements — we'll measure these ourselves against reference product photos Clear copy and microcopy — already written Fast feedback loop — we'll respond to questions within a few hours during business days Who we're looking for Required: 3+ years building custom Shopify themes or Shopify apps Strong JavaScript (vanilla or a framework like Alpine / Lit / React — we have no strong preference) Comfortable with canvas or SVG rendering for the mockup layer Experience with Shopify's Storefront API and line item properties Can work from a written spec and the provided prototype without needing the design re-explained Strongly preferred: Prior work on apparel decoration, print-on-demand, or product customizer tools Familiarity with the screen print / embroidery industry's pricing models Experience with SanMar, S&S, or similar wholesale supplier catalog structures Portfolio links to a live Shopify product customizer you've built Not required but nice: Experience with Supply Master or similar Shopify catalog sync tools Design sensibility strong enough to catch aesthetic details the spec doesn't explicitly call out How to apply Please include the following in your proposal — applications missing these will be skipped: Links to 2 or 3 live product customizer tools you've built (Shopify preferred, other platforms acceptable if the work is comparable) A brief description of how you'd architect the garment template system — specifically how you'd avoid the per-variant setup problem. One or two paragraphs is plenty. Your proposed stack — what JS framework/libraries you'd use and why, where artwork files would be stored, how you'd structure the admin-editable pricing config A realistic timeline estimate broken into milestones Whether you prefer fixed price or hourly, with your rate or quote Your availability — how many hours per week you can commit and when you can start One question you have about the project — this tells us how carefully you read the brief Skip generic cover letters. We'd rather see a short, specific proposal than a polished template. We'll give clear feedback and won't scope-creep mid-build. If we want something not in the original spec, we'll treat it as a change order. Final payment happens after the tool is live on our store and handling real customer orders successfully for 7 days — not at code delivery. Milestones before that can be structured however works for you. Look forward to seeing your proposal.

jobs.description: Hubspot optimization Go To Market Overview: Current Market Environment: 1) We process traditional sales led orders where reps use the Shopify admin website to create an order that is sent to a customer to approve/ pay. This comes back into hubspot using the free unific pipeline (Currently we use the free version so it's limited detail outside of dollar amount and customer name (not company name) 2) Reps can also set up a wholesale account and help customers place their first order in Shopify. Orders enter the unific pipeline 3) Stores can self service create orders inside Shopify, and those are currently flowing to HubSpot as deals in the unific pipeline Issues: We’re seeing duplicate deals which are inflating our pipeline. We're getting shipped deals in the unific ecommerce pipeline. Those are managed separately Reps are manually building deals for forecasting. However, closed/fulfilled orders are entering the system as deals via the Unific pipeline, which requires manual entry or updating of the forecasted deals. What should we do with the forecasted deals when a closed-won order appears in the Unific pipeline? We aren’t confident in the truth of the current data synchronization, particularly regarding sales revenue versus the reported value in deals/ pipeline (as evidenced by evaluating HubSpot dashboards against Shopify sales data). This is causing inaccuracy of forecasting in hubspot Our process is complex and harder to follow than it ideally should be - we need to simplify the current process/ workflow from managing multiple pipelines We are seeing “looping” of data with automation so all automation has been turned off Project Overview: We use HubSpot CRM to track deals, manage our sales pipeline, forecast future income, and track seller activity through notes, activities etc, We also use HubSpot to show current revenue (through deals closed won) and track historical revenue (closed won). Our process manages new business and recurring revenue through specific deal naming conventions (I assume there's a better way like tagging etc). We are using the free version of Unific (and evaluating their Quotific CPQ tool) to bridge our B2B and e-commerce workflows. Our sales team creates deals in HubSpot (the quotific tool would pivot that to us creating quotes -that push draft orders into Shopify). Today the unific connector pulls completed e-commerce orders from Shopify back into HubSpot as deals in a dedicated e-commerce pipeline, which is separate from our Hemp pipeline. We need a qualified Revenue Operations (RevOps) consultant to walk through our current state, identify structural bottlenecks, map out a clean architecture, and provide a best-practice operational overview to ensure accurate, non-duplicate reporting. Key Deliverables: System Audit & Discovery Walkthrough: Review the existing Unific mapping rules, HubSpot deal pipelines, and Quotific configurations. Pipeline Restructuring Plan: Outline clear delineations between quote-based forecasting and direct transactional e-commerce revenue. Deduplication & Loop Prevention Strategy: Formulate concrete logic to ensure a HubSpot quote that becomes a Shopify order does not sync back to HubSpot as a brand-new duplicate deal. Reporting & Forecasting Framework: Establish a best-practice strategy for custom reporting on closed-won revenue vs. rolling sales forecasts. Eliminate any multisystem loopback We are currently NOT connected to QuickBooks from HubSpot. We are only relying on Shopify to push information into QuickBooks, such as orders and customer information (today, this is mostly limited to contacts, not companies, even with the highest level of Shopify enabled). We also need to understand the optimal structure here. Direct Integration & Technical Questions "How do you handle 'loopback duplication' in HubSpot when a quote-turned-deal triggers a Shopify order, which then attempts to sync back into HubSpot as a second deal via Unific?" "What is your experience specifically with Quotific CPQ by Unific, and how do you ensure the draft order stages in Shopify correctly manipulate the forecast categories in HubSpot?" "Are you comfortable custom-mapping Shopify metafields or line-item properties through Unific to ensure granular data is clean on the HubSpot side?" Forecasting & Revenue Questions "How do you separate purely transactional, automated e-commerce sales from active B2B sales pipelines so that high-volume web orders don't break our human-led sales forecasts?" "To track revenue cleanly without cross-polluting past performance, do you recommend calculating revenue directly from Deal Amount properties or using HubSpot's line-item product library synced from Shopify?" "What are the specific HubSpot entry and exit criteria you would establish to automate a clean transition from 'Deal' to 'Order Fulfilled' across the systems?"
jobs.title: Hubspot Optimization for Revenue Management Integrated with Shopify

The trend isn't a one-off — it has continued consistently since then, and the client describes the pattern as "really distinct." The client's theory is that bot traffic is inflating their organic sessions and add-to-cart numbers without converting to real orders, which would explain both the traffic spike and the conversion drop happening together. jobs.description: Overview We are looking for an expert Shopify developer or agency to migrate a complex WooCommerce-based site to Shopify. This is not a basic migration. We are specifically looking for someone who: Has built high-converting Shopify stores with experience in revenue focus Understands AOV optimization, bundling, upsells, and post-purchase flows Has experience with conversion-focused landing pages (Meta / Google traffic) Ideally you have directly improved store performance through bundling, upsells, and post-purchase optimization, not just theme design. Current Stack WooCommerce (physical products) MemberPress (gated streaming content) Vimeo (video hosting behind paywall) FluentCRM (email + automation) Elementor (landing pages) ~500 SEO blog posts Amazon MCF fulfillment Coupon-based digital access (for Amazon customers) Core Objective Migrate to Shopify while improving conversion rate and AOV, not just replicating the current site. Scope of Work 1. Shopify Migration (Ecommerce) Rebuild product catalog (mats, towels, blocks, etc.) Preserve SEO structure Integrate Amazon MCF fulfillment Optimize PDPs for: Higher conversion rate Higher AOV 2. AOV Optimization (Critical) We expect strong implementation of: Bundling strategies (kits, “complete your practice”) In-cart upsells Post-purchase upsells (1-click) PDP cross-sells Subscription or repeat purchase opportunities (if applicable) This is a core part of the project—not an add-on. 3. Membership / Gated Content Replace MemberPress with a Shopify-compatible system: Users must be able to: Log in Access streaming video content (Vimeo embeds) Retain access to previously purchased content 4. Customer Migration (Critical) Migrate all users Preserve access to purchased content Support existing Amazon customers using coupon-based unlock system Password resets acceptable if necessary 5. Coupon-Based Access System We currently include physical coupon codes in Amazon orders. System must: Accept existing codes Unlock digital content Be scalable for future use 6. Email & CRM Migration Migrate from FluentCRM → Klaviyo Rebuild: Purchase flows Product-specific flows Lifecycle automation 7. SEO Migration Migrate ~500 blog posts Maintain rankings via: URL preservation or redirects Improve blog UX/design 8. Landing Pages (Meta / Google Traffic) We run (and plan to scale) paid traffic. We currently have about 15 landing pages and need them to be redesigned under new theme to be: High-converting landing pages (not generic templates) Clear CRO structure: Strong hooks Visual hierarchy Product education Conversion-focused layout 9. Migrate wordpress based quiz to new website. Bonus if you have experience with: Quiz funnels (Octane AI or similar) Design Direction Clean, minimal, premium Conversion-first (not just aesthetic) Shopify theme-based is fine, but must be customized for CRO Deliverables Fully functional Shopify site Membership/content system Migrated blog + redirects Klaviyo setup Amazon MCF integration Coupon unlock system Optimized PDPs + upsell flows Conversion-focused landing pages Ongoing Work (Preferred) We are looking for a partner for: CRO improvements AOV optimization Iteration on landing pages Ongoing development 10. Budget (important, must answer) Please provide: Rough fixed cost range based on the scope above Breakdown if possible: Migration Membership system CRO / AOV optimization Landing pages We understand final pricing may require review, but we want a real estimate—not “TBD after audit.” TO APPLY (REQUIRED) 1. Shopify CRO Examples (MANDATORY) Share 3 Shopify stores you’ve worked on that include: Bundling Upsells (cart or post-purchase) High-converting PDPs Landing pages for paid traffic Links required. 2. Performance Context For at least 1 example, include: What you improved (CVR, AOV, etc.) What you implemented (bundles, upsells, flows) 3. Migration Experience Have you migrated: WooCommerce → Shopify? Sites with gated content or memberships? Explain briefly. 4. Tech Approach Answer briefly: What tools would you use for: Memberships? Post-purchase upsells? Bundling? Landing pages? Quiz funnels? 5. Key Problem Solving Short answers: How would you preserve access for existing users? How would you handle SEO migration for ~500 posts? How would you rebuild a coupon-based unlock system? 6. Timeline + Cost Estimated timeline Rough cost range
jobs.title: Shopify Migration Expert with CVR, AOV, Funnels Focus (From WooCommerce/Memberpress/Elementor Stack)

jobs.description: Overview We are Velyx Research Ltd, a newly established UK limited company supplying research-grade peptides to the UK market. We are looking for an experienced Shopify freelancer to build a clean, professional, and fully compliant e-commerce store for our initial product launch of two compounds: BPC-157 and GHK-Cu. This is not a standard e-commerce build. Our industry has specific legal and compliance requirements that must be built into the website from the ground up. We need a freelancer who understands — or is willing to learn — the research chemical sector and can implement the necessary compliance features without cutting corners. What We Have Already Shopify account set up Brand colours selected Hero section designed Brand name: Velyx Logo: to be confirmed What We Need Built 1. Age Verification Gate A compliant age verification popup that appears before any visitor can access the site. This must: Confirm the visitor is 18 or over Confirm they understand products are sold for research purposes only Be impossible to bypass without confirmation Appear on every first visit and after cookies are cleared 2. Homepage Clean, professional, trust-focused design Brand colours and fonts applied consistently Hero section already started — needs integrating properly Clear "for research purposes only" framing throughout Trust signals: UK registered company, third-party tested, COA available No medical claims, no therapeutic language anywhere on the site 3. Product Pages — 2 Products to Start BPC-157 (5mg) GHK-Cu (50mg) Each product page must include: Clean product imagery Compliant product description (research use framing — we will provide copy) Certificate of Analysis (COA) display — either embedded PDF or downloadable link, batch specific Purity percentage display Clear pricing Add to cart functionality Prominent research use disclaimer on every product page 4. Certificate of Analysis (COA) Display System This is critical. Every product must display its current batch COA clearly. We need: A simple system to upload and update COAs per batch PDFs displayed or downloadable directly from the product page Batch number and test date visible 5. Essential Pages The following pages must be built and properly written or templated: About Us — who we are, our commitment to quality and compliance FAQ — covering what research peptides are, how to order, shipping, COAs Shipping Policy — UK shipping, cold chain handling, estimated delivery times Refund Policy — compliant with UK consumer law Terms of Service — including research use only clause Privacy Policy — GDPR compliant Contact Page — contact form and email address 6. Navigation Clean header navigation linking to Shop, About, FAQ, Contact Footer with full legal links, company registration number, disclaimer Full legal disclaimer in footer on every page 7. Checkout & Payments Checkout must include a tick box confirming the buyer acknowledges products are for research use only — this must be mandatory before purchase completes Payment provider integration — we will advise on provider once confirmed with a high-risk merchant processor Stripe may or may not be available for this sector — freelancer must have experience working with alternative payment gateways if needed 8. Legal & Compliance Copy Throughout the site the following language must be used consistently: "For research purposes only" "Not for human consumption" "Not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease" No therapeutic claims No before/after imagery No testimonials implying health benefits Design Direction Clean, minimal, and trustworthy — think clinical but approachable Not overly scientific or cold — we want it to feel credible but accessible Colour palette already established — will be shared with successful applicant Mobile responsive — must look excellent on phone and desktop Fast loading — page speed is important for SEO and user experience Technical Requirements Built on Shopify Mobile responsive SEO basics in place — page titles, meta descriptions, alt tags Google Analytics integration Fast page load speed GDPR cookie consent banner Secure checkout (SSL) Budget & Timeline Budget: Up to £500 Timeline: As soon as possible — ideally live within 2 weeks of project start We are ready to begin immediately upon finding the right freelancer. What We Are Looking For in a Freelancer Proven Shopify experience — please include examples of previous Shopify builds in your proposal Experience with compliance-sensitive industries (research chemicals, supplements, CBD, pharmaceuticals) is a strong advantage Understanding of UK e-commerce regulations Clear communicator — we want regular updates throughout the build Ability to work quickly without sacrificing quality Based in UK or Europe preferred but not essential. How to Apply Please include in your proposal: Links to 2–3 Shopify stores you have built previously A brief explanation of how you would approach the age verification and COA display requirements specifically Your realistic timeline to complete the full build Confirmation you are comfortable working within the research-use-only compliance framework Applications that do not address the compliance requirements specifically will not be considered. We are looking for someone who has read this brief carefully and understands what we are building.

jobs.description: Orthora is a fast-scaling DTC footwear brand built for workers who spend 8 to 12+ hours a day on their feet — nurses, teachers, warehouse workers, hospitality. We're doing $20K/day in revenue, scaling toward $100K/day, and our customers trust us with a purchase that literally carries them through their shift. That trust is earned at every touchpoint — and customer experience is where we win or lose it. THE ROLE We're hiring a Customer Experience Manager to own the full post-purchase experience end-to-end: support operations, dispute resolution, chargeback defense, team management, and AI-powered workflows that let us scale CX without scaling headcount linearly. You will not be handling tickets day-to-day (we have agents for that). You'll be building the system — training the team, designing the playbooks, owning the metrics, and winning back money we shouldn't be losing. WHAT YOU'LL OWN Support Operations. Manage a remote CX team (agents and VAs), own staffing, shift coverage, SLAs, and QA. Hit under 4hr first response, under 24hr resolution, 90 percent or higher CSAT. Dispute and Chargeback Defense. Own PayPal, Shopify Payments, Stripe, and credit card dispute responses. Build evidence templates, reduce chargeback rates, and recover every dollar that's recoverable. You know what "compelling evidence" actually means to each processor. Refund and Return Policy. Own the refund logic — when to approve, when to push back, when to offer store credit, when to save the sale. Reduce refund rate without tanking CSAT. Crisis Management. When we have a shipping delay, a sizing issue, or a supplier problem, you're the calm in the storm. Proactive outreach, coordinated messaging, damage control. AI Workflows. Deploy and tune Gorgias/Zendesk AI, macro libraries, sentiment routing, auto-tagging, and LLM-powered response drafting. Cut ticket handle time by 50 percent or more while maintaining quality. Reporting and Insights. Weekly CX dashboard to leadership: volume, resolution time, CSAT, refund rate, chargeback rate, top complaint categories. Turn ticket data into product, ops, and marketing feedback. Team Development. Hire, train, and develop a team of 5 to 15 CX agents across time zones. WHAT YOU'VE DONE BEFORE 10+ years in ecommerce customer experience, with at least 5 in a management or leadership role at a DTC brand. You've scaled a CX team through a period of rapid growth (from $1M to $10M+ ARR or equivalent). Deep fluency in dispute and chargeback workflows — PayPal, Shopify Payments, Stripe, Klarna, Afterpay. You've personally written and won chargeback responses and can quote win rates you've driven. Ran CX for a physical product brand — ideally footwear, apparel, or fashion where sizing, fit, and returns are core challenges. Expert in at least two helpdesks (Gorgias, Zendesk, Intercom, Kustomer, Freshdesk). Comfortable in Shopify admin (orders, refunds, fulfillment, tags). Strong written English — you'll be writing policies, responses, and customer-facing copy. AI FLUENCY — NON-NEGOTIABLE This is an AI-native role. You should be able to answer "how would you use AI to..." questions without hesitation: Deployed AI-powered ticket automation (Gorgias AI Agent, Zendesk AI, Intercom Fin, or custom LLM flows) at a previous company and can show measurable impact on deflection rate, handle time, or CSAT. Built macro and response libraries with LLMs to standardize quality across a team. Used AI for sentiment analysis, auto-tagging, and escalation routing. Comfortable prompting ChatGPT, Claude, or similar to build internal tools, analyze ticket trends, and draft responses. Bonus: experience with AI voice agents (Sierra, Ada, Decagon, Parloa) or building custom GPTs or Claude Projects for CX. If your answer to "how are you using AI in CX today?" is "a little bit of ChatGPT here and there," this role isn't for you. BONUS POINTS Experience with footwear, orthopedics, or healthcare-adjacent products. You've handled high-volume dispute fire — e.g. a viral product launch, a shipping disaster, a supplier issue — and know how to triage without burning out the team. Familiar with post-purchase platforms: Loop Returns, Returnly, AfterShip, Parcel Panel, Klaviyo for CX-triggered flows. Experience coordinating with 3PLs and international logistics (we ship globally). You've built a voice-of-customer program that fed insights back to product and marketing. WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE — FIRST 90 DAYS Day 30: Full audit of current CX ops — team, tools, policies, ticket data. Presented to leadership with prioritized gap list. Day 60: Chargeback response SOP live, dispute win rate measurably up, AI ticket automation deployed or tuned. Day 90: CX dashboard live, team structure optimized, CSAT trending up, refund rate trending down. LOGISTICS Remote, long-term contract with potential to transition to full-time leadership role. Competitive compensation based on experience. We invest in the stack you need to win.
jobs.title: Customer Experience Manager — DTC Footwear

jobs.description: Project Description I need a Magento 2 technical SEO specialist to diagnose why my site has severe indexing problems. This is a diagnostic-only job — I am not hiring for the fix in this engagement. I want a written report identifying the root causes, with specific recommendations, so I can make an informed decision about next steps. Current situation Magento 2.4.7-p4 on Nexcess hosting UK B2B ecommerce site (glass partition systems) Approximately 843 enabled products. Two product ranges (SP straight partitions and LP corner/L-shape partitions) have been consolidated using MageWorx Advanced Product Options into 28 and 16 products respectively (down from 392 and 223), with 301 redirects in place from the old URLs. A third range (TP T-shape, ~320 products) has not yet been consolidated. Several other product ranges (frameless, framed door, industrial/Crittall styles, hardware kits, and accessories) make up the remainder. Custom calculator/configurator as the primary purchase path 5,114 backlinks, on-page SEO score 78 (Ubersuggest) Only 34 organic visits over the last 30 days — site is effectively invisible in Google The specific problem Google Search Console shows: 29,486 pages indexed 2,366,207 pages NOT indexed, broken down as: 2,010,091 "Crawled — currently not indexed" 238,708 "Not found (404)" 71,696 "Duplicate without user-selected canonical" 35,263 "Blocked by robots.txt" 8,822 "Alternate page with proper canonical tag" 1,334 Server errors (5xx) 251 Page with redirect The not-indexed count has grown from ~413,000 in Sept 2025 to 2.36M today despite multiple rounds of remediation work (robots.txt updates, 301 redirects after product consolidation, canonical fixes, sitemap regeneration, removal requests). Fixes have not held. Something in the Magento configuration is generating URLs faster than they can be blocked. No manual actions. No security issues. What I need you to diagnose What is generating the 2M+ "Crawled — currently not indexed" URLs? Identify the specific Magento configuration, module, extension, or URL rewrite pattern responsible. I need the root cause, not a list of symptoms. Why are 238K URLs returning 404? These are likely from the product consolidation. Identify whether proper 301 redirects are in place and whether there are patterns that need addressing. Why are 71K pages showing as "Duplicate without user-selected canonical"? Diagnose the canonical tag configuration at the Magento level and identify why it's not resolving. What's causing the 1,334 server errors? Identify which URL patterns are returning 5xx responses and why. Are there Magento configuration issues (url_rewrite table bloat, layered navigation settings, configurable product duplication, category path settings, etc.) that are contributing to the problem? Review the current robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and canonical tag implementation for effectiveness. Deliverables A written report (PDF or Google Doc) containing: Root cause analysis for each of the six areas above Specific technical findings with examples (URLs, config settings, table entries, etc.) A prioritised list of recommended fixes with effort/impact estimates Your estimated cost and timeline to implement those fixes if I choose to hire you (or someone else) for the remediation work Loom video walkthrough of the key findings (preferred) What this job is NOT Not the fix. I may hire you for remediation afterward, or I may hire someone else, or I may decide to migrate platforms. This is a diagnostic to inform that decision. Not a generic SEO audit. I don't need a list of meta description recommendations or backlink suggestions. I need a Magento-specific technical diagnosis. Not a sales pitch for ongoing retainer work. Access I will provide Google Search Console (read-only) Google Analytics 4 (read-only) Magento admin access (restricted to required permissions) SFTP access if needed to inspect files Database read access if needed (via Nexcess) Timeline and budget Budget: £200 fixed price for the diagnostic report Timeline: completed within 10 days of hire I will consider proposals slightly above £200 if clearly justified by experience To be considered, please include in your proposal Confirmation you have worked on Magento 2 technical SEO specifically (not Shopify or WooCommerce). A brief example of a previous similar diagnostic you've done — one real example, with specifics (client details redacted is fine). Generic "I've done this many times" proposals will not be considered. The specific Magento areas/tables/configurations you would examine to diagnose URL explosion problems (this is my filter — a competent specialist will list things like url_rewrite table, catalog_url_rewrite, layered navigation configuration, configurable product URL handling, CMS page routing, etc.). How long you estimate the diagnostic will take. Whether you would be available to implement fixes afterwards, and a rough cost range for the remediation work based on what you've read above. I will not respond to generic proposals. Please address points 1–5 directly.
jobs.title: Magento 2 technical SEO diagnostic audit — indexing and URL generation issues

jobs.description: **Shopify Horizon Integrator — Mistricotine Redesign** Project type: Visual redesign on Shopify Horizon (no migration) Primary language: French (English secondary, already configured via Shopify Markets) Start: Within 3 to 4 weeks of selection --- ## About Mistricotine Mistricotine (mistricotine.com) is a French premium baby knitwear brand — merino wool, knitted in France, designed for the newborn wardrobe. Positioning centered on softness, craftsmanship and the birth-gift — with a strong focus on premium visual quality and editorial design. The site runs on Shopify Horizon with a curated, premium range — every product photographed in a Parisian studio, every collection editorial. The brand is founded and run by Sophie Delas. The team includes a photographer, an AI retouching designer, an illustrator, and now — you — the Shopify integrator. --- ## Context & working methodology We are redesigning the existing site — no migration. The instance, SKUs, inventory, orders and customer accounts stay in place. **The team has 3 members with strict roles:** - **Claude (AI)** generates all Liquid, JS vanilla, CSS and JSON-LD code. Each component is delivered annotated, ready to paste, with a detailed technical brief. Claude also generates guides, QA checklists and documentation. - **Sophie (project manager)** configures the theme editor (colors, fonts, content), creates metafields and 17 collections via the admin interface, configures Search & Discovery and Shopify Email, imports products and 301 redirects via native Shopify CSV, runs the project, executes cross-browser QA and validates every delivery visually. - **You (integrator)** integrate the provided code into the Horizon 3.5.1 DEV theme, test in the real environment, and debug integration conflicts. **You do not code from scratch.** Your role is to integrate, test and debug. --- ## Your exact role 1. Apply the Mistricotine CSS tokens (provided) to the Horizon 3.5.1 DEV theme — **mandatory first task** 2. Integrate Liquid sections (6 templates, code provided by Claude) 3. Integrate custom JS components (code provided) and test live 4. Debug JS integration conflicts between apps and theme 5. Integrate Schema.org + canonicals into theme.liquid (code provided) 6. Integrate Judge.me Liquid snippet into PDP + Home (code provided) 7. Integrate Alma widget into PDP + payment sticky bar (code provided) 8. Verify Search & Discovery filters display correctly live (Sophie configures the S&D interface) 9. Execute Core Web Vitals corrections live (action plan provided by Claude) 10. Fix WCAG AA bugs reported by Sophie (VoiceOver + keyboard navigation tests) 11. Fix bugs reported by Sophie during cross-browser QA --- ## Scope of work ### 1. Template integration (6 templates — Liquid code provided) **HOME** — Video hero + animated ticker + USP strip + press strip + bundles + Offrir Mistricotine + featured pieces + annotated know-how + birth list (static editorial section) + reviews + newsletter + payment bar + footer + exit intent JS + sticky CTA **COLLECTION** — Hero + active filters + swatch hover + sticky bar + wishlist + product grid + SEO content + related collections *(FAQ Teaser: out of scope — handled by Sophie via theme editor)* **PRODUCT PAGE (PDP)** — Gallery + ATC + embroidery field JS + sticky ATC bar + tabs + complete the look + upsell + reviews **BLOG HOME** — Editorial hero + featured article + category navigation **ARTICLE** — Reading progress bar + share buttons + inline product block **NOTRE HISTOIRE (About)** — Editorial storytelling sections ### 2. Technical setup - **Schema.org** — JSON-LD integration in theme.liquid (code provided) - **Canonical tags FR/EN** — Liquid snippet integration (code provided) - **Search & Discovery** — verify filters display correctly live (Sophie configures the interface) - **301 redirects** — Sophie imports the CSV in Admin → Navigation → URL Redirects (Claude generates the file) ### 3. Apps - **Judge.me Reviews** — Liquid snippet PDP + Home (code provided) — free plan - **Shopify Email** — Sophie configures entirely in the interface, no dev required - **Alma** — 3x payment widget PDP + bar (code provided) — no subscription, commission on sales only ### 4. Performance - Sophie runs Lighthouse → Claude generates action plan → you execute corrections and measure live - Preload fonts, lazy load, defer scripts (snippets provided) ### 5. Accessibility - Sophie tests VoiceOver + keyboard navigation (protocol provided by Claude) - You fix WCAG AA bugs reported (aria attributes, focus states, contrast) ### 6. QA & handover - Sophie runs cross-browser QA with Claude’s checklist - You fix reported bugs - Claude generates full documentation — Sophie records Loom walkthroughs --- ## What we provide **At day 1:** - Access to Horizon 3.5.1 DEV theme (staff account, theme permissions only) - Mistricotine CSS tokens file to apply at kick-off - Brand guide (palette, typography, tone of voice) - All technical briefs per component - Step-by-step guides per phase **Delivered progressively, before each phase:** - Liquid section files (annotated, ready to paste) - JS vanilla components (ticker, exit intent, embroidery, sticky ATC, progress bar) - JSON-LD Schema.org + canonical snippet - Judge.me snippet + Alma integration code - 301 redirects CSV (native Shopify format) - QA checklist + VoiceOver protocol --- ## Expected deliverables - CSS tokens applied on Horizon 3.5.1 DEV theme - 6 templates integrated and functional - Zero JS console errors - Lighthouse mobile score ≥ 85 - WCAG AA on PDP, ATC flow and checkout entry - Judge.me + Alma integrated and tested with real data - QA bugs fixed --- ## Out of scope — Phase 2 post-launch **Birth list (functional)** — customization of Horizon native wishlist (renamed, PDP button, shareable link). To evaluate after launch based on customer demand. Estimated: 2–3h. --- ## Technical requirements Required: - Shopify Horizon v3.x (non-negotiable) - Liquid 5, JSON templates, Shopify 2.0 sections/blocks architecture - Metafields, Shopify Markets - Debugging JS conflicts between apps and theme Nice to have: - French proficiency - Experience with premium D2C brands - Experience with Judge.me or Alma on Shopify Not required: design, full-stack development, React, Next.js, headless Shopify, Search & Discovery configuration, 301 redirects import. --- ## Estimated scope **20–25 hours total.** Async workflow, task-by-task handoff. Target rate: max $35/h (equivalent to 30 €/h). | Phase | Duration | Deliverable | |---|---|---| | 1. Kick-off | 1 week | CSS tokens applied, DEV theme configured | | 2. Templates | 4–6 weeks | 6 templates integrated and validated | | 3. Apps + SEO | 2–3 weeks | Judge.me + Alma + Schema.org + canonicals | | 4. Performance + QA | 2–3 weeks | CWV ≥ 85, WCAG AA, bugs fixed | | 5. Post-launch | +1 week | Minor fixes | Total estimated duration: 10–15 weeks. No hard deadline — quality over speed. --- ## How to apply Applications without these 5 elements will not be reviewed. **1. Portfolio** — 2 to 3 live Shopify Horizon projects (verifiable links). **2. Methodology note (half page)** — how you handle a redesign on an existing Shopify instance without breaking inventory or checkout, and how you work async with a non-developer project manager. **3. Answers to these 3 questions:** - Have you worked with Shopify Horizon v3.x specifically? If yes, share a store URL or describe the project. - Describe a JS conflict between a Shopify app and a theme you debugged. What was the problem and how did you solve it? - Do you have experience with Judge.me or Alma on Shopify? **4. Quote** — fixed price preferred with breakdown per phase. Hourly acceptable if you estimate total hours with confidence. **5. Availability** — start date and weekly hours committed. We will shortlist 3 candidates within one week, schedule a 30-minute call, and select within 10 days.

jobs.description: Project Description I run a UK-based B2B ecommerce business (glass partitions) on Magento 2.4.7. I'm currently spending approximately £450/month on Google Ads but can't tell whether it's producing results because my conversion tracking is broken. I need a Google Ads specialist to audit the account, fix the tracking, remove waste, and hand it back working properly. Current situation Magento 2.4.7-p4 ecommerce site Google Analytics 4 is installed Google Ads account has multiple conversion actions, most are broken or not firing "Purchase" conversion flagged as "Needs attention" — 0 conversions recorded in 30 days despite real sales happening "Calls from ads" conversion active but recording 0 calls in 30 days (customers do call after clicking ads) A legacy Universal Analytics conversion action is still present and needs removing Most of my customers call or request a quote rather than buy online — offline conversion visibility is poor I don't have a developer on retainer, but I have full admin access to Magento, Google Ads, Google Analytics, and Google Tag Manager (if installed) What I need you to do Audit current conversion tracking across Google Ads and Google Analytics 4. Identify what's firing, what's broken, what's duplicate, and what's dead. Fix online purchase tracking so that every completed Magento order (including bank transfer orders, which go through Magento checkout but don't take payment immediately) is recorded as a conversion in Google Ads, with the correct order value passed through. Set up phone call conversion tracking so that calls from people who clicked an ad are recorded. Minimum threshold 60 seconds to count as a lead. Clean up the conversion actions list — remove the Universal Analytics action, remove any duplicate or redundant conversions, and designate one clear primary conversion for Purchase and one for Phone Call. Review campaign settings, search terms, and keywords. Add negative keywords to exclude irrelevant searches. Identify whether any campaigns are structurally misconfigured. Set up a simple reporting dashboard (in Google Ads or Looker Studio) that shows me, at a glance: spend, clicks, online conversions, call conversions, total conversion value, and ROAS. Provide a written handover covering what you changed, how the tracking now works, and what I should monitor going forward. Loom video walkthrough preferred. What I do NOT need Ongoing management. This is a one-off fix. Website redesign or SEO work (that's separate). Anyone who wants to push me straight into Performance Max without fixing tracking first. Access I can provide Google Ads admin access (via email invite) Google Analytics 4 admin access Magento admin access (to install/verify tags if needed) Google Tag Manager access if relevant Timeline and budget Target completion: within 7–10 days of hire Budget: £200–£400 fixed price for the full scope above Happy to pay more for a clearly experienced specialist with verifiable results To be considered, please include in your proposal Confirmation that you've set up Magento 2 → Google Ads conversion tracking before (specifically, not just Shopify or WooCommerce) How you'll verify the tracking is working end-to-end after you set it up (I want to understand your testing process) Whether you use Google Tag Manager or direct tag installation, and which you'd recommend for my situation and why An example of a previous similar job (screenshots or case study, with client details redacted is fine) Your proposed fixed price for the full scope Please do not send a generic proposal. I'll prioritise proposals that directly address points 1–5.
jobs.title: Google Ads audit and conversion tracking fix — Magento 2 B2B ecommerce site
